functions.definition: addClass(elm: Element, classNames: string | string[]): Element
functions.comment: Adds one or multiple class names to a DOM element
examples:
addClass(MyNode, 'active') // --> <div class="active" />
addClass(MyNode, ['active', 'open']) // --> <div class="active open" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : The given elm

functions.definition: append(elm: Element, insertElm: string | Node): Element
functions.comment: Append DOM element or plain HTML to the end of a given DOM element
examples:
append(MyNode, NodeToAppend)
append(MyNode, '<span>appended</span>')
append(MyNode, '.my-appended-element')
functions.descriptions: @returns : The inserted child element

functions.definition: attr(elm: Element, attrName: string, value?: string | number | boolean): string
functions.comment: Get/set the value of an attribute on a given DOM element
examples:
// Get the value of an attribute
attr(document.documentElement, 'lang'); // --> eg. 'en-US'

// Set the value of an attribute
attr(document.documentElement, 'lang', 'da-DK'); // --> <html lang="da-DK">

// Set a boolean value
attr(MyInput, readonly, true); // --> <input readonly />
attr(MyInput, readonly, false); // --> <input />
functions.descriptions: @returns : Data found in the attribute (the old value if {value} is defined)

functions.definition: boxModel(elm: Element): BoxModelMapping
functions.comment: Parses the box model numbers of an given Element (margin, padding and border widths)
examples: boxModel(someDiv);
functions.descriptions: @returns : The mapping of the box model

functions.definition: children(elm: ParentNode): Element[]
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found child DOM elements

functions.definition: classNameString(args: ClassNamesInput[]): string
functions.comment: Create a string of names that will be used as class names for a given element.
examples:
classNameString('my-elm', 'open', 'active') // --> "my-elm open active"
classNameString(['my-elm', 'open', 'active']) // --> "my-elm open active"
classNameString('my-elm', ['open', 'active']) // --> "my-elm open active"
classNameString('my-elm', { open: true, active: true }) // --> "my-elm open active"
functions.descriptions: @returns : The list of class names (eg. "my-elm open active")

functions.definition: contentBoxSize(element: Document | GeneralWindow | HTMLElement): Size
functions.comment:
Find the size of a DOM element, document or window excluding borders, margins and padding
(Getting the size of the viewport if document or window is given)
examples:
// <div style="width: 200px; height: 300px; margin: 10px; border: 2px solid;" />
contentBoxSize(div) // --> { width: 200, height: 300 }

contentBoxSize(window) // --> [size of the viewport]
contentBoxSize(document) // --> [size of the viewport]
functions.descriptions: @returns : Object describing width and height of the element

functions.definition: contentSize(element: Document | GeneralWindow | HTMLElement): Size
functions.comment:
Find the size of the content (scrollable area minus padding) of a DOM element, document or window
(Getting the size of the viewport if document or window is given)
examples:
// <div style="width: 200px; height: 200px; margin: 10px; border: 2px solid; overflow: scroll">
//   <div style="height: 400px; width: 400px" />
// </div>
contentSize(div) // --> { width: 400, height: 400 }

contentSize(window) // --> [size of the viewport]
contentSize(document) // --> [size of the viewport]
functions.descriptions: @returns : Object describing width and height of the element

functions.definition: copyEvent<Textends Event>(event: T, currentTarget?: EventTarget): T
functions.comment: Copies a given event and optionally changes the currentTarget
examples: copyEvent(SomeEvent, MyNewCurrentTargetElement)
functions.descriptions: @returns : The copied event

functions.definition: createElement(selector?: string): Element
functions.comment: Creates an element from a given CSS selector (restricted to only one element)
examples:
// Create a div
create(); // --> <div />

// Create an element
create('img'); // --> <img />

// Create an element from a selector
create('#MyElement.active'); // --> <div id="MyElement" class="active" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : The created element

functions.definition: css(elm: HTMLElement): CSSStyleDeclaration
functions.comment: Get the computed styling of a DOM element.
examples: css(MyElm) // --> All computed styles of MyElm
functions.descriptions: @returns : All styling on the element

functions.definition: css(elm: HTMLElement, property: string): string | number
functions.comment:
Gets a given inline style property on a DOM element.
Return values that are pure numbers or pixel values will be converted to pure number first
examples:
css(MyElm, 'font-size') // --> 30
css(MyElm, 'borderSize') // --> '1px'
functions.descriptions: @returns : All styling on the element or the value of the given style property

functions.definition: css(elm: HTMLElement, styles: CSSStyleProperties): CSSStyleDeclaration
functions.comment:
Set multiple inline styling properties on a DOM element.

Notes on values:

  • null as value, removes the given property
  • !important in the value will be parsed and set correctly (eg. font-size: 15px !important)
examples: css(MyElm, { fontSize: 30, 'font-weight': 'bold !important' }) // --> All computed styles of MyElm
functions.descriptions: @returns : All styling on the element

functions.definition: css(elm: HTMLElement, property: string, value: string | number): CSSStyleDeclaration
functions.comment:
Set a given inline style property on a DOM element.

Notes on values:

  • null removes the given property
  • !important added to the value, it will be parsed and set correctly
examples: css(MyElm, 'fontSize', 30) // --> All computed styles of MyElm
functions.descriptions: @returns : All styling on the element or the value of the given style property

functions.definition: domReady(handler: () => void): void
functions.comment: Execute a given function once the document has finished loading
examples: domReady((e: Event) => { alert('DOM Ready') });

functions.definition: docComplete(): boolean
functions.comment: returns whether document ready state indicates that the document is ready to be manipulated

functions.definition: elementData(elm: Node, key?: string, data?: unknown): unknown
functions.comment:
Inspired by jQuery's data method that stores data in memory associated with a given element.

  • data = undefined: Return the stored data for the given key
  • data = null: Remove the stored data for the given key
  • key = undefined: return the entire data object
examples:
elementData(MyElm, 'entry', { count: 10 }) // --> { count: 10 }
elementData(MyElm, 'entry') // --> <What ever was stored under "entry">
elementData(MyElm, 'entry', null) // --> delete the "entry" entry
elementData(MyElm) // --> everything was stored for the given element
remarks: Data is stored in a WeakMap, so when an element is deleted, the associated data is deleted as well.
functions.descriptions: @returns : The entire cache or the data for the given key

functions.definition: resetCache(): void
functions.comment: Resets ALL local data cache

functions.definition: elmIndex(elm: Element): number
functions.comment: Find the index of a DOM element amongst its siblings
examples: elmIndex(someDiv); // --> 3
functions.descriptions: @returns : The index of elm

functions.definition: ensureHTML(htmlOrSelector: string): string
functions.comment:
Parses a string to see if it is already HTML otherwise it assumes
is a selector and parses it to HTML
examples:
// String is already HTML so it is returned as is
ensureHTML('<div />');

// String is a selector, to is is parsed before it is returned
ensureHTML('.my-div'); // --> <div class="my-div" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : The HTML string

functions.definition: find<Textends HTMLElement>(selector: string): T | T[]
functions.comment:
Find elements by a given selector. The selector will be lightly analyzed to determine
the appropriate findByXX function. This should be faster than just running querySelector(All)
to find elements.
examples:
find('#my-id') // Uses getElementById()
find('.my-class') // Uses getElementsByClassName()
find('div') // Uses getElementsByTagName()
find('#my-id.my-class') // Uses querySelectorAll()
find('#my-id > .my-class + p') // Uses querySelectorAll()
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: find<Textends HTMLElement>(elm: Document | HTMLElement, selector: string): T | T[]
functions.comment:
Find elements by a given selector from a given element. The selector will be lightly analyzed to determine
the appropriate findByXX function. This should be faster than just running querySelector(All)
to find elements.
examples:
find(myElm, '#my-id') // Uses myElm.querySelectorAll()
find(myElm, '.my-class') // Uses myElm.getElementsByClassName()
find(myElm, 'div') // Uses myElm.getElementsByTagName()
find(myElm, '#my-id.my-class') // Uses myElm.querySelectorAll()
find(myElm, '#my-id > .my-class + p') // Uses myElm.querySelectorAll()
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findByClass<Textends HTMLElement>(classNames: string | string[]): T[]
functions.comment:
Finds DOM elements with a given class name.
Separate multiple selectors by comma. Separate multiple class names by space.
examples:
findByClass('my-id') // --> all ".my-elm" elements
findByClass('my-id, my-other-elm') // --> all ".my-elm" and ".my-other-elm" elements
findByClass('my-id my-other-elm') // --> all ".my-elm.my-other-elm" elements
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findByClass<Textends HTMLElement>(elm: Document | HTMLElement, classNames: string | string[]): T[]
functions.comment:
Finds DOM elements with a given class name from a given element.
Separate multiple selectors by comma. Separate multiple class names by space.
examples:
findByClass(myElm, 'my-id') // --> all ".my-elm" elements as descendant of myElm
findByClass(myElm, 'my-id, my-other-elm') // --> all ".my-elm" and ".my-other-elm" elements as descendant of myElm
findByClass(myElm, 'my-id my-other-elm') // --> all ".my-elm.my-other-elm" elements as descendant of myElm
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findById<Textends HTMLElement>(id: string): T
functions.comment: Find a DOM element with the given ID
examples:
findById('my-id') // --> "#my-id" element
findById('non-existing') // --> null
functions.descriptions: @returns : The found element

functions.definition: findById<Textends HTMLElement>(ids: string[]): T[]
functions.comment: Find a DOM elements from a list of IDs
examples:
findById(['my-id', 'my-other-id']) // --> "#my-id" and "#my-other-elm" elements
findById(['non-existing', 'non-existing-2']) // --> []
functions.descriptions: @returns : The found elements

functions.definition: findByName<Textends HTMLElement>(names: string | string[]): T[]
functions.comment: Find DOM elements with the given name
examples:
findByName('my-element-name') // --> all "[name=my-element-name]" elements
findByName(['my-element-name', 'my-second-name']) // --> all "[name=my-element-name]" and "[name=my-second-name]" elements
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findByQuery<Textends HTMLElement>(elm: Document | HTMLElement, queries: string | string[]): T[]
functions.comment: Find all elements matching a given CSS selector from a given element
examples: findByQuery(MyElm, 'span.my-class') // --> All "span.my-class" elements that are descendants of MyElm
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findByQuery<Textends HTMLElement>(queries: string | string[]): T[]
functions.comment: Find all elements matching a given CSS selector
examples: findByQuery('span.my-class') // --> All "span.my-class" elements that are descendants of document
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findOneByQuery<Textends HTMLElement>(elm: Document | HTMLElement, query: string): T
functions.comment: Find first elements matching a given CSS selector from a given element
examples: findOneByQuery(MyElm, 'span.my-class') // --> First "span.my-class" elements that are descendants of MyElm
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findOneByQuery<Textends HTMLElement>(query: string): T
functions.comment: Find first elements matching a given CSS selector
examples: findOneByQuery('span.my-class') // --> First "span.my-class" elements that are descendants of document
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findByTagName<Textends HTMLElement>(tagNames: string | string[]): T[]
functions.comment: Find elements by given tag name
examples: findByTagName('div') // --> All <div> elements
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: findByTagName<Textends HTMLElement>(elm: Document | HTMLElement, tagNames: string | string[]): T[]
functions.comment: Find elements by given tag name
examples: findByTagName(MyElm, 'div') // --> All <div> elements that are descendants of MyElm
functions.descriptions: @returns : List of found DOM elements

functions.definition: getCurrentDocument(node: Node | Document | GeneralWindow): Document
functions.comment: Determines the relevant owner document object from a give node
examples:
getCurrentDocument(document) // --> `document` directly
getCurrentDocument(window) // --> `window.document`
getCurrentDocument(myNode) // --> The `document` in which myNode resides
functions.descriptions: @returns : The owner document (if can be determined)

functions.definition: getCurrentWindow(node: Node | Document | GeneralWindow): GeneralWindow
functions.comment: Determines the relevant owner window object from a give node
examples:
getCurrentWindow(document) // --> `window` the document belongs to
getCurrentWindow(window) // --> `window` directly
getCurrentWindow(myNode) // --> The `window` in which myNode resides
functions.descriptions: @returns : The owner window (if can be determined)

functions.definition: hasClass(elm: Element, classNames: string | string[], any?: boolean): boolean
functions.comment: Does all (or any) of the listed class names exist in the DOM elements list of class names
examples:
hasClass(myElm, 'open') // --> true if myElm has the "open" class name
hasClass(myElm, ['open', 'shown']) // --> true if myElm has some or all given class names
hasClass(myElm, ['open', 'shown'], true) // --> true if myElm has all given class names
functions.descriptions: @returns : All/any class names listed were found in the elements list of class names

functions.definition: hasAllClass(elm: Element, classNames: string | string[]): boolean
functions.comment: Does all of the listed class names exist in the DOM elements list of class names
examples:
hasAllClassNames(myElm, 'open') // --> true if myElm has the "open" class name
hasAllClassNames(myElm, ['open', 'shown']) // --> true if myElm has all given class names
functions.descriptions: @returns : All of the class names listed were found in the elements list of class names

functions.definition: hasAnyClass(elm: Element, classNames: string | string[]): boolean
functions.comment: Does any of the listed class names exist in the DOM elements list of class names
examples:
hasAnyClassName(myElm, 'open') // --> true if myElm has the "open" class name
hasAnyClassName(myElm, ['open', 'shown']) // --> true if myElm has at least one of the given class names
functions.descriptions: @returns : At least one of the class names listed were found in the elements list of class names

functions.definition: hidden(elm: HTMLElement): boolean
functions.comment:
Test if a given DOM element is technically hidden

  • Not in DOM
  • Collapsed (no width and height)
  • display: none
  • visibility: hidden.
examples:
hidden(myNormalElement) // --> false
hidden(myZeroWidthAndHeightElement) // --> true
hidden(myNoDisplayElement) // --> true
hidden(myNoVisibilityElement) // --> true
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is the element technically hidden or not

functions.definition: inDOM(elm: Node): boolean
functions.comment: Is the given DOM node inserted into the DOM
examples:
inDOM(document.getElementById('my-elm')) // --> true
inDOM(document.createElement('div')) // --> false
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a DOM node in the DOM or not

functions.definition: innerSize(element: Document | GeneralWindow | HTMLElement): Size
functions.comment:
Find the size of a DOM element, document or window excluding borders, margins and scrollbars.
Getting the size of the viewport if document or window is given.
examples:
// <div style="width: 200px; height: 300px; margin: 15px; padding: 10px; border-width: 2px;" />
innerSize(div) // --> { width: 220, height: 320 }

innerSize(window) // --> [size of the viewport]
innerSize(document) // --> [size of the viewport]
functions.descriptions: @returns : Object describing width and height of the element

functions.definition: innerXML(elm: Element): string
functions.comment:
Gets the inner XML structure as a string from a XML/HTML element
(like innerHTML but also for XML elements - eg. in SVG)
examples:
innerXML(<div><span>my text</span></div>); // --> '<span>my text</span>'
innerXML(<svg><g><path /></g></svg>); // --> '<g><path /></g>'
functions.descriptions: @returns : The inner XML structure

functions.definition: insertAfter(elm: Element, insertElm: string | Element): Element
functions.comment:
Inserts DOM element or plain HTML after a given DOM element
(not possible for detached elements or the element)
examples:
insertAfter(document.documentElement, myInsertElm) // --> null (not possible)
insertAfter(document.createElement('div'), myInsertElm) // --> null (not possible)

insertAfter(myElm, myInsertElm) // --> myInsertElement
insertAfter(myElm, '<div />') // --> <div />
insertAfter(myElm, '.inserted-element') // --> <div class="inserted-element" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : The inserted element

functions.definition: insertBefore(elm: Element, insertElm: string | Element): Element
functions.comment:
Inserts DOM element or plain HTML before a given DOM element
(not possible for detached elements or the element)
examples:
insertBefore(document.documentElement, myInsertElm) // --> null (not possible)
insertBefore(document.createElement('div'), myInsertElm) // --> null (not possible)

insertBefore(myElm, myInsertElm) // --> myInsertElement
insertBefore(myElm, '<div />') // --> <div />
insertBefore(myElm, '.inserted-element') // --> <div class="inserted-element" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : The inserted element

functions.definition: inView(elm: HTMLElement, threshold?: number): PositionIndicator
functions.comment: Determines whether the element is in the area of the viewport or not.
examples:
// Element inside viewport
const { inside } = inView(myElement); // --> inside === true

// Element outside viewport
const { inside, below } = inView(myElement); // --> inside === false; below === true

// With a threshold
const { inside } = inView(myElement, 30); // --> inside === true
functions.descriptions: @returns : An object with indications of where the element is compared to the viewport area

functions.definition: invisible(elm: HTMLElement): boolean
functions.comment:
Test if a given DOM element is invisible.

Itself or a parent is:

  • hidden
  • No width or no height
  • opacity: 0
examples:
invisible(document.body) // --> false
invisible(MyNormalElement) // --> false
invisible(myZeroHeightElement) // --> true
invisible(myZeroWidthElement) // --> true
invisible(myNoDisplayElement) // --> true
invisible(myNoVisibilityElement) // --> true
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is the element invisible

functions.definition: isBlob(obj: unknown): boolean (obj is Blob)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a Blob object or not

functions.definition: isDocument(obj: unknown): boolean (obj is Document)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a DOM document node or not

functions.definition: isDOMChildNode(node: unknown): boolean (node is ChildNodeWithParent)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a DOM child node or not

functions.definition: isDOMContainer(obj: unknown): boolean
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a DOM container or not

functions.definition: isDOMElement(node: unknown): boolean (node is Element)
functions.comment: Is the given object a DOM element node and optionally of a given type
examples:
isDOMElement(document.createElement('p')) // --> true
isDOMElement(document.documentElement) // --> true
isDOMElement(document.body) // --> true

isDOMElement(window) // --> false
isDOMElement(document.createTextNode('')) // --> false
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a DOM element node or not and optionally of the right type

functions.definition: isDOMFragment(obj: unknown): boolean (obj is DocumentFragment)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a Document Fragment or not

functions.definition: isDOMNode(node: unknown): boolean (node is Node)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a DOM node or not

functions.definition: isDOMRoot(node: unknown): boolean (node is HTMLElement)
functions.comment: Is the given object root node of the DOM (eg <html> for HTML documents og <svg> for SVG documents)
examples:
isDOMRoot(document.documentElement) // --> true

isDOMRoot(document.body) // --> false
isDOMRoot(document) // --> false
isDOMRoot(window) // --> false
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it the root node of the DOM or not

functions.definition: isEventTarget(obj: unknown): boolean (obj is EventTarget)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it an Event Target or not

functions.definition: isHTMLChildElement(node: unknown): boolean (node is HTMLChildElement)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a child HTMLElement or not

functions.definition: isHTMLElement(obj: unknown): boolean (obj is HTMLElement)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a HTML element node

functions.definition: isWindow(obj: unknown): boolean (obj is GeneralWindow)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is it a Window object or not

functions.definition: marginBoxSize(element: Document | GeneralWindow | HTMLElement): Size
functions.comment:
Find the size of a DOM element, document or window including margins and borders
(Getting the size of the viewport if document or window is given)
examples:
// <div style="width: 200px; height: 300px; margin: 15px; padding: 10px; border-width: 2px;" />
marginBoxSize(div) // --> { width: 254, height: 354 }

marginBoxSize(window) // --> [size of the viewport]
marginBoxSize(document) // --> [size of the viewport]
functions.descriptions: @returns : Object describing width and height of the element

functions.definition: nextSiblings(elm: Element): Element[]
functions.comment: Get all sibling elements after a given DOM element in the structure
examples: nextSiblings(someElement);
functions.descriptions: @returns : Collection of sibling elements

functions.definition: off<Eextends string>(elm: EventTarget, eventNames: E | E[], handler: EventHandler<E>, options?: AddEventListenerOptions): EventTarget
functions.comment: Bind an event handler for one or more event names on a given DOM element.
examples:
off(MyElm, 'click', () => {})
off(MyElm, 'click', () => {}, { passive: true })
off(MyElm, ['mouseenter', 'touchstart'], () => {})
functions.descriptions: @returns : elm

functions.definition: off<Eextends string>(eventNames: E | E[], handler: EventHandler<E>, options?: AddEventListenerOptions): Document
functions.comment: Bind an event handler for one or more event names to document
examples:
off('click', () => {})
off('click', () => {}, { passive: true })
off(['mouseenter', 'touchstart'], () => {})
functions.descriptions: @returns : document

functions.definition: on<Eextends string>(elm: EventTarget, eventNames: E | E[], handler: EventHandler<E>, options?: OnOptions<E>): () => EventTarget
functions.comment: Bind an event handler for one or more event names on a given DOM element.
examples:
const removeEvent = on(MyElm, 'click', someHandler)
const removeEvent = on(MyElm, 'click', someHandler, { passive: true })
const removeEvent = on(MyElm, ['mouseenter', 'touchstart'], someHandler)

When using options.when

let allowClick = false;
const removeEvent = on(MyElm, 'click', someHandler, { when: () => allowClick });

// In this case the `click` event won't trigger unless "allowClick" is `true`

When using options.delegate

const removeEvent = on(MyElm, 'click', someHandler, { delegate: '.click-me' });

// Here we listen to the `click` on MyElm, but the event will only trigger
// if the clicked target is a child of `MyElm` and matches the given selector ('.click-me').
// `this` and currentTarget, will match the current ".click-me" element

A combination of options

const removeEvent = on(MyElm, 'click', someHandler, {
  delegate: '.click-me',
  when: () => allowClick,
  once: true
});

// In this scenario a MyElm '.click-me' child element will trigger the event handler,
// but only if the condition of `when` returns true ("allowClick" is `true`) and the
// event will trigger only once (event will be removed when the conditions for when
// and delegate have been fulfilled).
functions.descriptions: @returns : function to remove added event handlers

functions.definition: on<Eextends string>(eventNames: E | E[], handler: EventHandler<E>, options?: OnOptions<E>): () => Document
functions.comment: Bind an event handler for one or more event names on document.
examples:
const removeEvent = on('click', someHandler)
const removeEvent = on('click', someHandler, { passive: true })
const removeEvent = on(['mouseenter', 'touchstart'], someHandler)

When using options.when

let allowClick = false;
const removeEvent = on('click', someHandler, { when: () => allowClick });

// In this case the `click` event won't trigger unless "allowClick" is `true`

When using options.delegate

const removeEvent = on('click', someHandler, { delegate: '.click-me' });

// Here we listen to the `click` on but the event will only trigger
// if the clicked target matches the given selector ('.click-me').
// `this` and currentTarget, will match the current ".click-me" element

A combination of options

const removeEvent = on('click', someHandler, {
  delegate: '.click-me',
  when: () => allowClick,
  once: true
});

// In this scenario a '.click-me' child element will trigger the event handler,
// but only if the condition of `when` returns true ("allowClick" is `true`) and the
// event will trigger only once (event will be removed when the conditions for when
// and delegate have been fulfilled).
functions.descriptions: @returns : function to remove added event handlers

functions.definition: outerSize(element: Document | GeneralWindow | HTMLElement): Size
functions.comment:
Find the size of a DOM element, document or window including borders.
(Getting the size of the viewport if document is given)
examples:
// <div style="width: 200px; height: 300px; margin: 10px; border: 2px solid;" />
outerSize(div) // --> { width: 224, height: 324 }

outerSize(document) // --> [size of the viewport]
functions.descriptions: @returns : Object describing width and height of the element

functions.definition: parseSelector(selector: string): SelectorParsing
functions.comment: Parses CSS a selector string into a structured object
functions.descriptions:
@returns : The attribute parsing mapping

parseSelector('.my-elm[name=test]') // --> { tagName: 'div', attributes: { name: 'test', class: 'my-elm' }, attributeList: ['name', 'class'] }
parseSelector('input[type=submit]') // --> { tagName: 'input', attributes: { type: 'submit' }, attributeList: ['type'] }

functions.definition: position(elm: HTMLElement): PositionData
functions.comment: Get the current absolute position of a DOM element and optionally also the relative position (position relative to offset parent)
examples:
// Returns a 0 position
position(null);

// Basically returns the size of the element, as top and left will be 0
position(document.documentElement);
position(document.body);

// Get the position of a given element
position(MyElm);
functions.descriptions: @returns : the position information of the element

functions.definition: prepend(elm: Element, insertElm: string | Node): Element
functions.comment: Append DOM element or plain HTML to the beginning of a given DOM element
examples:
prepend(MyNode, NodeToPrepend)
prepend(MyNode, '<span>prepended</span>')
prepend(MyNode, '.my-prepended-element')
functions.descriptions: @returns : The inserted child element

functions.definition: previousSiblings(elm: Element): Element[]
functions.comment: Get all sibling elements before a given DOM element in the structure
examples: previousSiblings(someElement);
functions.descriptions: @returns : Collection of sibling elements

functions.definition: removeClass(elm: Element, classNames: string | string[]): Element
functions.comment: Remove one or multiple class names from a DOM element
examples:
// <div class="active open" />
removeClass(MyNode, 'active') // --> <div class="open" />
removeClass(MyNode, ['active', 'open']) // --> <div class="" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : Returns element given in 'elm'

functions.definition: replaceClass(elm: Element, classNames: string | string[], replacements: string | string[]): Element
functions.comment: Replaces css class with another on a DOM element.
examples:
// <div class="active open" />
replaceClass(MyNode, 'active', 'inactive') // --> <div class="inactive open" />
replaceClass(MyNode, ['active', 'open'], ['inactive', 'closed']) // --> <div class="inactive closed" />
replaceClass(MyNode, 'active', ['inactive', 'removed']) // --> <div class="inactive removed open" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : Returns element given in 'elm'

functions.definition: replaceNode(elm: Node, replacement?: string | Node): void | Node
functions.comment: Replace a given DOM element with another DOM element or plain HTML string
examples:
// <div><span id='a' /></div>
replaceNode(document.getElementById('a'), document.createElement('p')) // --> <div><p /></div>
replaceNode(document.getElementById('a'), 'p.p') // --> <div><p class="p" /></div>
functions.descriptions: @returns : The value given in elm

functions.definition: scrollInfo(elm?: Element | Document | GeneralWindow): ScrollInfo
functions.comment: Gather the current scroll position information of a DOM element or the window
examples:
scrollInfo(window)
scrollInfo(document)
scrollInfo(MyElm)
functions.descriptions: @returns : The scroll information

functions.definition: scrollParent(elm: Element): Element | HTMLElement
functions.comment: Get the parent element that has scrolling
examples:
scrollParent(document.body) // --> `<html>`

// <div style="overflow:auto"><p id="P" /></div>
scrollParent(document.getElementById('P')) // --> `<div>`

// <div style="overflow:auto"><p id="P" style="position: fixed" /></div>
scrollParent(document.getElementById('P')) // --> `<html>`

// <div style="overflow:auto"><p id="P" style="position: absolute" /></div>
scrollParent(document.getElementById('P')) // --> `<html>`

// <div style="overflow:auto; position: relative"><p id="P" style="position: absolute" /></div>
scrollParent(document.getElementById('P')) // --> `<div>`
functions.descriptions: @returns : The scroll parent or the viewport

functions.definition: selectorToHTML(selector: string): string
functions.comment:
Converts a given CSS selector into HTML
(No this is not an Emmet substitute, so it is limited to only one element)
examples: selectorToHTML('#id.class-name'); // --> '<div id="id" class="class-name" />'
functions.descriptions: @returns : The parsed HTML

functions.definition: siblings(elm: Element): Element[]
functions.comment: Get all sibling elements of a given DOM element
examples: siblings(someElement);
functions.descriptions: @returns : Collection of sibling elements

functions.definition: toDOM(html: string): HTMLCollection
functions.comment: Convert HTML into DOM node(s)
examples:
// HTML cannot be parsed easily
toDOM('<html><body><div /></body></html>'); // --> null

// Convert a given HTML string to HTML
toDOM('<div><a>link</a></div>')
// --> <div><a>link</a></div>
remarks:
To keep the method simple and short, this method uses the <template />
element to convert the HTML. For older browsers, either
use a polyfill like @webcomponents/template or a more elaborate HTML parser
(like parsehtml or html-parser)
functions.descriptions: @returns : DOM elements that the HTML represented

functions.definition: toggleClass(elm: Element, classNames: string | string[], force?: boolean): Element
functions.comment: Toggles (add/remove) one or multiple class names on a DOM element
examples:
// <div class="active open" />
toggleClass(MyNode, 'active') // --> <div class="open" />
toggleClass(MyNode, ['active', 'open']) // --> <div class="active" />

// <div class="active open" />
toggleClass(MyNode, 'active', true) // --> <div class="active open" />
toggleClass(MyNode, 'active', false) // --> <div class="open" />
functions.descriptions: @returns : The given elm

functions.definition: trigger(elm: EventTarget, eventNames: string | string[], data?: unknown): EventTarget
functions.comment: Trigger one or more events on a given DOM element.
examples:
trigger(MyElm, 'click')
trigger(MyElm, 'my-event', { SomeEntry: true })
functions.descriptions: @returns : The 'elm' (or document)

functions.definition: trigger(eventNames: string | string[], data?: unknown): Document
functions.comment: Trigger one or more events on Document.
examples:
trigger('click')
trigger('my-event', { SomeEntry: true })
functions.descriptions: @returns : The 'elm' (or document)

functions.definition: uniqueNodeList<Textends Element>(args: (T | T[] | NodeListOf<T> | HTMLCollectionOf<T>)[]): T[]
functions.comment:
Make a unique list of Elements from on or multiple Element collections
(usually as a result of some element selection method)
examples:
const byClassName = (selector) => document.getElementsByClassName(selector)

// Using a single selector
findUniqueNodeCollection('my-elements', byClassName);

// Using multiple selectors
findUniqueNodeCollection(['.my-element', 'sone-other-elements'], byClassName);
functions.descriptions: @returns : The unique list of elements

functions.definition: vendorPrefixed(str: string): VendorPrefixing
functions.comment: Add vendor prefixes to a string
examples:
vendorPrefixed('backface-visibility')
// [
//   { prefix: '', js: 'backfaceVisibility', css: 'backface-visibility' }
//   { prefix: 'webkit', js: 'webkitBackfaceVisibility', css: '-webkit-backface-visibility' }
//   { prefix: 'moz', js: 'mozBackfaceVisibility', css: '-moz-backface-visibility' }
//   { prefix: 'ms', js: 'msBackfaceVisibility', css: '-ms-backface-visibility' }
//   { prefix: 'o', js: 'oBackfaceVisibility', css: '-o-backface-visibility' }
// ]
functions.descriptions: @returns : Array of the various vendor vendorPrefixed versions of the string

functions.definition: viewport(elm?: Element | Document | GeneralWindow): HTMLElement
functions.comment: Get the current viewport element (scrolling element) of the current document, from a given element
examples:
// Get the viewport of the current document
viewport();

// Get the viewport of the current window
viewport(window);

// Get the viewport of a given element
viewport(someElementInSomeDocument);
functions.descriptions: @returns : The viewport element

functions.definition: visible(elm: HTMLElement): boolean
functions.descriptions: @returns : Is the element visible for the user

functions.definition: wrap(elm: Element, wrapping: string | Element): Element
functions.comment: Wrap a given element in the given HTML, selector or element
examples:
wrap(document.documentElement, '<div />') // --> false - you cannot wrap the <html> element

wrap(MyElm, document.createElement('div')) // --> true
wrap(MyElm, '<div class="wrap-element"><span><b /></span></div>') // --> true - element inserted into the <b> tag
wrap(MyElm, '.wrap-element') // --> true
functions.descriptions: @returns : If the wrapping was successful or not

customTypes.name: CamelCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown:
type CamelCaseSettings = PhrasifySettings & {
  // Keep abbreviations uppercase (false == HTMLElement => HtmlElement | true == HTMLElement => HTMLElement)
  abbr?: boolean
  // Convert to UpperCase CamelCase (aka PascalCase)
  upper?: boolean
};

customTypes.name: ChunkStringOptions
customTypes.markdown:
type ChunkStringOptions = {
  // Chunk strings from the end to front (['ab', 'cde', 'fgh'] instead of ['abc', 'def', 'gh'])
  reverse?: boolean
  // The max size of each chunk
  size?: number
};

customTypes.name: DebounceCallback
customTypes.markdown: type DebounceCallback = (args: unknown[]) => void;

customTypes.name: DebouncedFunction
customTypes.markdown:
type DebouncedFunction = T & {
  // Method to cancel the current debounce
  cancelDebounce: () => void
};

customTypes.name: CurrencyFormatter
customTypes.markdown: type CurrencyFormatter = (num: number) => string;

customTypes.name: FormatNumberSettings
customTypes.markdown:
type FormatNumberSettings = {
  // The decimal separator character
  decimal?: string
  // How many decimals to show
  decimalCount?: number | string
  // The thousand separator character
  thousand?: string
};

customTypes.name: FuzzySearchOptions
customTypes.markdown:
type FuzzySearchOptions = {
  // Respect the case of the input, or not
  caseSensitive: boolean
};

customTypes.name: KebabCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown: type KebabCaseSettings = PhrasifySettings;

customTypes.name: PascalCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown: type PascalCaseSettings = Omit<CamelCaseSettings, 'upper'>;

customTypes.name: PhrasifySettings
customTypes.markdown:
type PhrasifySettings = {
  // Make numbers the delimeter of a word or not
  numbers?: boolean
};

customTypes.name: PromisefiedFunction
customTypes.markdown: type PromisefiedFunction = (args: any[]) => Promise<any>;

customTypes.name: PromisefyCallback
customTypes.markdown: type PromisefyCallback = (args: any[]) => any;

customTypes.name: RGBTuple
customTypes.markdown: type RGBTuple = [red: number, green: number, blue: number, alpha?: number];

customTypes.name: SnakeCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown: type SnakeCaseSettings = PhrasifySettings;

customTypes.name: TruncateSettings
customTypes.markdown:
type TruncateSettings = {
  // What ending to give the truncated string (default is "...")
  end?: string
  // Max length of the given string
  maxLength?: number
};

customTypes.name: BoxModelMapping
customTypes.markdown:
type BoxModelMapping = {
  // The element’s border widths (top, left, bottom, right).
  border: BoxModelSectionMapping
  // The element’s margin sizes (top, left, bottom, right).
  margin: BoxModelSectionMapping
  // The element’s padding sizes (top, left, bottom, right).
  padding: BoxModelSectionMapping
};

customTypes.name: BoxModelSectionMapping
customTypes.markdown:
type BoxModelSectionMapping = {
  // The size (in pixels) of the bottom side.
  bottom: number
  // The size (in pixels) of the left side.
  left: number
  // The size (in pixels) of the right side.
  right: number
  // The size (in pixels) of the top side.
  top: number
};

customTypes.name: ClassNamesInput
customTypes.markdown: type ClassNamesInput = SimpleClassNameInput | ClassNamesInput[];

customTypes.name: SimpleClassNameInput
customTypes.markdown: type SimpleClassNameInput = string | Record<string, boolean | undefined>;

customTypes.name: CamelCaseCSSStyleDeclarationKeys
customTypes.markdown: type CamelCaseCSSStyleDeclarationKeys = { [K in CSSStyleDeclarationKeys]?: string | number };

customTypes.name: CamelToKebab
customTypes.markdown: type CamelToKebab = S extends `${infer T}${infer U}` ? U extends Uncapitalize<U> ? `${Lowercase<T>}${CamelToKebab<U>}`: `${Lowercase<T>}-${CamelToKebab<U>}`: S;

customTypes.name: CSSProperty
customTypes.markdown: type CSSProperty = keyof CSSStyleProperties;

customTypes.name: CSSStyleDeclarationKeys
customTypes.markdown: type CSSStyleDeclarationKeys = { [K in keyof CSSStyleDeclaration]: CSSStyleDeclaration[K] extends (args: unknown[]) => unknown ? never: K }[keyof CSSStyleDeclaration];

customTypes.name: CSSStyleProperties
customTypes.markdown: type CSSStyleProperties = CamelCaseCSSStyleDeclarationKeys & KebabCaseCSSStyleDeclarationKeys;

customTypes.name: KebabCaseCSSStyleDeclarationKeys
customTypes.markdown: type KebabCaseCSSStyleDeclarationKeys = { [K in CSSStyleDeclarationKeys]?: string | number };

customTypes.name: Args
customTypes.markdown: type Args = [elm: Maybe<Document | HTMLElement>, queries: string | string[]];

customTypes.name: OneArgs
customTypes.markdown: type OneArgs = [elm: Args[0], query: string];

customTypes.name: PositionIndicator
customTypes.markdown:
type PositionIndicator = {
  // Above the viewport area?
  above: boolean
  // Below the viewport area?
  below: boolean
  // Inside the viewport area?
  inside: boolean
  // To the left of the viewport area?
  left: boolean
  // To the right of the viewport area?
  right: boolean
};

customTypes.name: ChildNodeParentProps
customTypes.markdown: type ChildNodeParentProps = 'parentElement' | 'parentNode';

customTypes.name: ChildNodeWithParent
customTypes.markdown: type ChildNodeWithParent = Omit<ChildNode, ChildNodeParentProps> & { [K in ChildNodeParentProps]: HTMLElement[K] };

customTypes.name: HTMLChildElement
customTypes.markdown: type HTMLChildElement = Omit<HTMLElement, HTMLElementParentProps> & { [K in HTMLElementParentProps]: HTMLElement[K] };

customTypes.name: HTMLElementParentProps
customTypes.markdown: type HTMLElementParentProps = 'offsetParent' | 'parentElement' | 'parentNode';

customTypes.name: OnOptions
customTypes.markdown:
type OnOptions = AddEventListenerOptions & {
  // A selector that defines which element is the actual target of the event
  delegate?: string
  // A method that returns true when the event should trigger
  // Combined with `once`, it will only remove the handler when `when()` resolves to true)
  when?: (event: ActualEvent<E>) => boolean
};

customTypes.name: AttributeMapping
customTypes.markdown:
type AttributeMapping = Record<string, string | 'true'> & {
  // Class name(s) of the tag
  class?: Set<string>
};

customTypes.name: SelectorParsing
customTypes.markdown:
type SelectorParsing = {
  // List of attributes on the tag
  attributeList: string[]
  // Record of the tags final attributes
  attributes: Record<string, string>
  // Current tag name
  tagName: string
};

customTypes.name: Position
customTypes.markdown:
type Position = {
  // Number of pixels from the top to the bottom side of the element
  bottom: number
  // Number of pixels from the left
  left: number
  // Number of pixels from the left to the right side of the element
  right: number
  // Number of pixels from the top
  top: number
};

customTypes.name: PositionData
customTypes.markdown:
type PositionData = Position & {
  // Position relative to the offset parent
  relative?: Position
};

customTypes.name: ScrollInfo
customTypes.markdown:
type ScrollInfo = {
  // The horizontal scroll position
  x: number
  // The maximum horizontal scroll position
  xMax: number
  // The horizontal scroll percentage
  xPct: number
  // The vertical scroll position
  y: number
  // The maximum vertical scroll position
  yMax: number
  // The vertical scroll percentage
  yPct: number
};

customTypes.name: ActualEvent
customTypes.markdown: type ActualEvent = E extends keyof DocumentEventMap ? DocumentEventMap[E]: CustomEvent<E>;

customTypes.name: EventHandler
customTypes.markdown: type EventHandler = (this: EventSource | EventTarget, event: ActualEvent<E>) => unknown;

customTypes.name: EventName
customTypes.markdown: type EventName = keyof DocumentEventMap | string;

customTypes.name: GeneralWindow
customTypes.markdown: type GeneralWindow = Window | window;

customTypes.name: Maybe
customTypes.markdown: type Maybe = T | null | undefined;

customTypes.name: NotFirst
customTypes.markdown: type NotFirst = T extends [arg0: T[0], rest: infer R] ? R: never;

customTypes.name: Size
customTypes.markdown:
type Size = {
  // Height in pixels
  height: number
  // Width in pixels
  width: number
};

customTypes.name: VendorMicrosoft
customTypes.markdown:
type VendorMicrosoft = {
  // The microsoft prefix for CSS properties
  css: `-ms-${string}`
  // The microsoft prefix for JS methods
  js: `ms${string}`
  // The microsoft prefix name
  prefix: 'ms'
};

customTypes.name: VendorMozilla
customTypes.markdown:
type VendorMozilla = {
  // The mozilla prefix for CSS properties
  css: `-moz-${string}`
  // The mozilla prefix for JS methods
  js: `moz${string}`
  // The mozilla prefix name
  prefix: 'moz'
};

customTypes.name: VendorNone
customTypes.markdown:
type VendorNone = {
  // CSS property without prefix
  css: string
  // JS method without prefix
  js: string
  // No prefix needed
  prefix: ''
};

customTypes.name: VendorOpera
customTypes.markdown:
type VendorOpera = {
  // The opera prefix for CSS properties
  css: `-o-${string}`
  // The opera prefix for JS methods
  js: `o${string}`
  // The opera prefix name
  prefix: 'o'
};

customTypes.name: VendorPrefixing
customTypes.markdown: type VendorPrefixing = [VendorNone, VendorWebkit, VendorMozilla, VendorMicrosoft, VendorOpera];

customTypes.name: VendorWebkit
customTypes.markdown:
type VendorWebkit = {
  // The webkit prefix for CSS properties
  css: `-webkit-${string}`
  // The webkit prefix for JS methods
  js: `webkit${string}`
  // The webkit prefix name
  prefix: 'webkit'
};
